UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user within the application, holding their profile information and assigned role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically matching the Firebase Auth UID.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, determining access permissions. (enum: Admin, Dispatcher, Viewer)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Site.title: Site
Site.description: Represents a construction site where material deliveries are made.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Site entity.

name.description: The name of the construction site (e.g., 'โครงการ ABC สุขุมวิท 50').

address.description: The physical address of the construction site.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the site, typically picked via a map click.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the site, typically picked via a map click.

projectTypeTag.description: A tag indicating the primary type of project at this site. (enum: Electrical, Plumbing, HVAC, Mixed)

status.description: The operational status of the site. (enum: Active, Inactive)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the site was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the site was last updated.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Represents a delivery vehicle used for transport.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vehicle entity.

licensePlate.description: The unique license plate number of the vehicle.

type.description: The classification or type of the vehicle. (enum: Pickup, 4-wheel truck, 6-wheel truck)

maxLoadCapacityKg.description: The maximum permissible load capacity of the vehicle in kilograms.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the vehicle record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the vehicle record was last updated.

Driver.title: Driver
Driver.description: Represents a driver responsible for operating a vehicle for deliveries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Driver entity.

name.description: The full name of the driver.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the driver record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the driver record was last updated.

CompanySetting.title: CompanySetting
CompanySetting.description: Stores global configuration settings for the LOTUS EME company, such as warehouse location and API key references.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CompanySetting entity. Expected to be a singleton (e.g., 'default' or 'company').

companyName.description: The official name of the company (e.g., 'LOTUS EME').

warehouseName.description: The name of the primary company warehouse or office location.

warehouseAddress.description: The physical address of the primary company warehouse or office.

warehouseLatitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the primary warehouse.

warehouseLongitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the primary warehouse.

googleMapsApiKeyReference.description: A reference or identifier to where the Google Maps API key is securely stored (e.g., Firebase environment config ID), not the key itself.

defaultMapCenterLatitude.description: The default latitude coordinate for centering maps in the application (e.g., Bangkok).

defaultMapCenterLongitude.description: The default longitude coordinate for centering maps in the application (e.g., Bangkok).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the settings were created or first configured.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the settings were last updated.

Trip.title: Trip
Trip.description: Represents a planned or executed material delivery trip, coordinating vehicles, drivers, and multiple stops.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Trip entity.

tripDate.description: The scheduled date for the delivery trip.

driverId.description: Reference to the Driver assigned to this trip. (Relationship: Driver 1:N Trip)

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle assigned to this trip. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N Trip)

departureSiteId.description: Reference to the departure Site for the trip, typically the warehouse. (Relationship: Site 1:N Trip)

stopIds.description: An ordered list of references to TripStop entities belonging to this trip. The order determines the route sequence. (Relationship: Trip 1:N TripStop)

status.description: The current operational status of the trip. (enum: Planned, In Progress, Completed, Cancelled)

totalDistanceKm.description: The calculated total distance for the entire trip in kilometers, typically from Google Routes API.

totalEstimatedTimeMinutes.description: The calculated total estimated travel time for the entire trip in minutes, typically from Google Routes API.

routePolyline.description: An encoded polyline string representing the full optimized route on a map, generated by Google Routes API.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the trip was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the trip was last updated (e.g., status change, route re-optimization).

TripStop.title: TripStop
TripStop.description: Represents a single delivery stop within a larger trip, detailing the site and cargo.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TripStop entity.

tripId.description: Reference to the Trip this stop belongs to. (Relationship: Trip 1:N TripStop)

siteId.description: Reference to the Site where this stop is located. (Relationship: Site 1:N TripStop)

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this stop within the trip (0-indexed).

plannedCargoDescription.description: Description of cargo items planned for delivery at this stop (e.g., 'สายไฟ 3 ม้วน, ท่อ PVC 50 เมตร').

actualCargoDescription.description: Description of cargo items actually delivered at this stop, editable after trip completion.

distanceFromPreviousKm.description: The calculated distance from the previous stop in the route, in kilometers.

estimatedTimeFromPreviousMinutes.description: The calculated estimated travel time from the previous stop in the route, in minutes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the trip stop was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the trip stop was last updated (e.g., cargo notes edited).

definition.description: Stores user profiles, including their unique ID (matching Firebase Auth UID), email, name, and crucial role (Admin, Dispatcher, Viewer) for role-based access control. The `userId` parameter represents the Firebase Authentication UID.
Stores details for each construction site, including name, address, GPS coordinates, project type, and status. Access is governed by the user's role (Admin/Dispatcher for CRUD, Viewer for Read).

params.name: siteId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific construction site.

definition.description: Manages delivery vehicle information such as license plate, type, and max load capacity. Access is governed by the user's role (Admin/Dispatcher for CRUD, Viewer for Read).

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific vehicle.

definition.description: Contains details for each driver, including name and phone number. Access is governed by the user's role (Admin/Dispatcher for CRUD, Viewer for Read).

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific driver.

definition.description: A singleton collection for global company-wide settings like warehouse location and API key references. Expected `settingId` is 'default'. Admin users have CRUD access, Dispatcher/Viewer users have Read-only access.

params.name: settingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific company setting document (e.g., 'default').

definition.description: Manages individual delivery trip details, including assigned driver and vehicle, stops, route information, and status. Access is granted to Admin/Dispatcher roles for CRUD, Viewer for Read, and the `driverId` assigned to the trip can read and update their own trips.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific delivery trip.

definition.description: A subcollection representing individual stops within a specific trip. Includes denormalized `driverId` (copied from the parent Trip) for Authorization Independence. Access is granted to Admin/Dispatcher roles for CRUD, Viewer for Read, and the `driverId` assigned to the parent trip can read and update their own trip stops.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent trip to which this stop belongs.

params.name: tripStopId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific stop within the trip.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs. The central tenet for authorization is the `UserProfile` document (`/userProfiles/{userId}`), which stores the user's `role` (Admin, Dispatcher, Viewer) based on the DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control) principle. Firebase Security Rules will leverage this `role` to grant or deny access.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Top-Level Collections:** Collections like `userProfiles`, `sites`, `vehicles`, `drivers`, and `companySettings` are designed to be entirely authorization-independent. Access rules for these collections will directly reference the `request.auth.uid` to fetch the user's role from their `UserProfile` document (e.g., `get(/userProfiles/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role`). This eliminates any hierarchical authorization dependencies between these top-level entities themselves.
2.  **`Trip` Collection:** `Trip` documents (`/trips/{tripId}`) are secured by either the user's `role` (Admin/Dispatcher for full access, Viewer for read-only) or by checking if `request.auth.uid` matches the `resource.data.driverId` field directly within the `Trip` document. This avoids `get()` calls for parent documents and enables drivers to access their assigned trips independently.
3.  **`TripStop` Subcollection (CRITICAL Denormalization):** To ensure Authorization Independence for `TripStop` documents (`/trips/{tripId}/tripStops/{tripStopId}`), the `driverId` from the parent `Trip` will be denormalized and copied into every `TripStop` document. This is a critical application of Strategy A. This denormalization allows `TripStop` security rules to operate solely on `resource.data.driverId` (or the user's global role), thereby completely eliminating the need for `get(/trips/$(tripId))` calls within the `TripStop` rules. This makes `TripStop` operations atomic, rules simple, and highly debuggable.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Homogeneous Security Posture:** Collections like `/sites`, `/vehicles`, `/drivers`, and `/companySettings` maintain a homogeneous security posture. All documents within these collections are accessible based on the user's global `role`. For instance, an `Admin` or `Dispatcher` can perform CRUD operations on *all* `Site` documents, while a `Viewer` can only read *all* `Site` documents. This directly supports secure `list` operations without requiring complex server-side filtering logic within rules. The client queries the full collection, and the rule simply validates access to the entire set.
2.  **User-Specific Data (`Trips`, `TripStops`):** For collections like `/trips` and `/trips/{tripId}/tripStops`, where access might be restricted to an assigned `driverId`, client-side queries must explicitly include filters (e.g., `db.collection('trips').where('driverId', '==', request.auth.uid)`). The Firebase Security Rules will then *validate* that `request.auth.uid` matches `resource.data.driverId` for each document being accessed or listed, in addition to checking for `Admin`/`Dispatcher` roles. The rules *do not filter* results; they enforce that *any requested data* (individual or via list query) matches the allowed access pattern. The denormalized `driverId` in `TripStop` is key here for QAPs on subcollections.

**Structural Segregation & Consistency:**
*   Entities are mapped to top-level collections (e.g., `/sites`, `/vehicles`).
*   Hierarchical paths are used for nested, logically dependent data (`/trips/{tripId}/tripStops/{tripStopId}`).
*   A dedicated `status` field is used for explicit state modeling across `Site` and `Trip` entities, simplifying rules based on document lifecycle. Wildcards in paths are descriptive (e.g., `{userId}`, `{siteId}`). The prompt's requirement for fixing non-functional CRUD for sites is directly addressed by enabling `Admin` and `Dispatcher` roles to perform these actions on the `/sites` collection, with authorization based on the user's role retrieved from `/userProfiles/{userId}`.
